title: Corallodiscus labordei
intro: Corallodiscus labordei, also known as Laborde's Corallodiscus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Corallodiscus labordei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Filipino medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used as a component in various herbal remedies, such as cough syrups and expectorants.
taxonomy: Corallodiscus labordei is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant belongs to the order Lamiales and is classified as a type of flowering plant. The class is Magnoliopsida, indicating that it is a dicotyledonous plant. At the kingdom level, Corallodiscus labordei is classified as Plantae. Its classification reflects its close relationship to other plants within the Gesneriaceae family.
